title: Natural and social science professionals n.e.c.
description: Analyze statistical data, such as mortality, accident, sickness, disability, and retirement rates and construct probability tables to forecast risk and liability for payment of future benefits. May ascertain insurance rates required and cash reserves necessary to ensure payment of future benefits. Conduct research in fundamental mathematics or in application of mathematical techniques to science, management, and other fields. Solve problems in various fields using mathematical methods. Formulate and apply mathematical modeling and other optimizing methods to develop and interpret information that assists management with decisionmaking, policy formulation, or other managerial functions. May collect and analyze data and develop decision support software, services, or products. May develop and supply optimal time, cost, or logistics networks for program evaluation, review, or implementation. (Based on 26 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Conduct research to gather information about survey topics, develop research studies, and design or maintain databases.
Analyze and interpret statistical data to identify significant differences and evaluate the statistical methods used for validity and accuracy.
Prepare, organize, and analyze large amounts of data for statistical modeling and graphic analysis, including reporting results in various formats.
Construct probability tables for events by analyzing statistical data and ascertaining necessary cas reserves for future benefits.
Collaborate with others to develop and improve quantitative methods and statistical approaches for various projects and fields.
Develop, test, and document mathematical or simulation models of problems and validate their results through computations and analyses.
Plan and direct research or survey projects, determining methodologies, sample sizes, and evaluating their progress and results.
Disseminate research findings by writing reports, publishing papers, and presenting at professional conferences.
Provide technical support, quality monitoring, and training as needed in laboratory environments, including supervising data collection staff.
Write proposals or grant applications, review research literature, and maintain knowledge in specific scientific or statistical fields.
Calibrate, validate, or maintain laboratory equipment, ensuring operational efficiency and reliability.
Identify quality problems and recommend solutions or improvements to enhance analytical methods and procedures.
Monitor testing procedures and complete necessary documentation to support testing, including data capture and equipment logbooks.
Interpret and validate test results, making recommendations on data appropriateness for release.
Compile and analyze laboratory test data and conduct routine and non-routine analyses of various samples.
Train and supervise other analysts in laboratory procedures and techniques, ensuring quality standards are met.
Develop specialized software routines for image analysis and integrate remotely sensed data with geospatial data.
Collect and analyze biological and geospatial data to support environmental and genetic research initiatives.
Research and develop new methodologies and protocols for genetic applications, and evaluate the performance of new technologies.
Conduct studies to investigate animal behavior, nutrition, and management practices to improve production efficiency and product quality.
Write technical reports and publish findings in scientific journals to communicate research results to the scientific community.
Investigate relationships between organisms and diseases, and recommend preventive methods and treatments.
Develop public health programs and conduct health safety assessments to improve community health outcomes.
Represent the organization at conferences and collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to enhance project outcomes.
Maintain accurate laboratory records, monitor data quality and ensure compliance with relevant regulations in research methodologies.
Conduct applied research aimed at improvements in disease testing, pharmaceuticals, and environmental management.
Identify and analyze public health issues related to foodborne parasitic diseases and their impact on public policies, scientific studies, or surveys.
Consult with and advise physicians, educators, researchers, and government officials regarding medical applications of sciences, such as physics, biology, and chemistry.
Evaluate food processing and storage operations and assist in the development of quality assurance programs for such operations.
Study methods to improve aspects of foods, such as chemical composition, flavor, color, texture, nutritional value, and convenience.
Develop new or improved ways of preserving, processing, packaging, or delivering foods using knowledge of chemistry, microbiology, and other sciences.
Inspect food processing areas to ensure compliance with government regulations and standards for sanitation, safety, and quality.
Plan and direct studies to investigate human or animal disease, preventive methods, and treatments for disease.
Teach or advise undergraduate or graduate students, or supervise their research.
Register and report incidents of infectious diseases to local and state health agencies, and educate public about communicable disease prevention.
Develop new products and procedures for sterilization, food preservation, or microbial contamination detection.
Analyze large datasets relevant to biological activities, such as genomic or proteomic data for clinical or basic research purposes.
Design and apply bioinformatics algorithms and develop data models and databases for scientific projects.
Investigate soil problems and analyze effects of management practices on soil and land productivity.
Conduct experiments to develop new or improved varieties of crops focusing on characteristics such as yield and disease resistance.

emerging_tasks.task: Analyze data to determine premium rates required and cash reserves and liabilities necessary to ensure payment of future benefits.
Operate remote sensing equipment on drones to collect data in areas that are difficult to access or require high-resolution imagery.
Perform tests on water, food, and the environment to detect harmful microorganisms or to obtain information about sources of pollution, contamination, or infection and interpret the results.
Teach epidemiology to students in public health programs.
Test processing equipment to ensure products are produced according to specifications.
Use advanced technologies, such as GIS, remote sensing, and drone technology, for wildlife tracking, habitat mapping, and population studies.
